title: santolina chamaecyparissus var. incana
intro: Santolina chamaecyparissus var. incana, also known as Grey Cotton Lavender or Woolly Grey Santolina, is a perennial shrubby plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its silvery-gray foliage and small yellow flowers. The leaves and flowers of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a valuable remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of Santolina chamaecyparissus var. incana include its ability to soothe digestive problems, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, which can help combat infections and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to make medicinal remedies, such as a stomach-calming tea or a topical cream for skin issues.
